1.Name: ゲームパッド
1.Title: ゲームパッドコントロール

2.Name: Keyboard
2.Title: キーボードコントロール

3.value:
Use the touchpad at the bottom left side of the screen to move.
 Tap it once to jump.
 This can also be configured to use directional buttons instead of the pad.
 


 Use the touchpad at the bottom right side of the screen to look around.
 Tap it once to jump.
 You can also drag your finger across any area of the screen to look.
 


 You can tweak control scheme of the game in the Controls and Sensitivity settings.
 Experiment with various options, for example you may find you prefer buttons to touchpads, or split touch with crosshair to regular controls.
 Controlling a first person game on a small touchscreen may look difficult, but once you get the hang of it, it is no different than using a gamepad or a mouse.
 Because you can touch any part of the screen at any time, some actions like block digging or placement may even be easier.
 


 Some cheap or old devices have low quality touch screens or buggy touch drivers.
 They might intermittently drop active touch points, or mix them up if touched in more than one point at once.
 These are best avoided as playing on them is extremely frustrating.
3.Title: タッチスクリーンコントロール

4.value:
Sneaking is useful if you want to move slowly and carefully, for example to avoid falling near the edge of a tall crevice.
 


 To toggle sneak mode, press the sneak button on the right side of the screen.
 When sneak mode is enabled, your movement will be restricted at edges to prevent you from falling.
 


 Sneaking also makes your movements much quieter, allowing you to come closer to certain animals without scaring them away. 
 For example, you can use sneak to get close to a bird without scaring it away. 
 Just make sure you don't approach it from the front, because it will see you and fly away.
4.Title: スニーク

5.value:
Flight is only available in Creative Mode.
 To toggle fly mode, tap the flight button on the right side of the screen. 
 Jumping twice in quick succession will also toggle fly mode.
 


 Flying is much faster than walking and allows you to quickly get to otherwise inaccessible areas.
5.Title: 飛行中

6.value:
Ladders can be crafted from wooden sticks.
 Once placed on a vertical wall, they allow you to climb the wall easily.
 


 It's safest to climb the ladder when facing it.
 This is true both for climbing upwards and downwards.
 


 Consequently, the best way to climb down a ladder is to approach it backwards, as you would do in real life.
 


 You can jump off the ladder at any time
6.Title: はしごを登っています

7.value:
Health bar is displayed in the game screen as little hearts.
 It is the amount of life left in the player.
 When it drops to zero, the player dies.
 


 It slowly replenishes when player is not hungry, it replenishes faster when sleeping.
 Be careful when your health is low, even a slight injury may kill you.
 


 When the player is sick, the hearts become dirty green.
 Do not eat dodgy food to avoid sickness.
 When the player has flu, the hearts become blue.
 Do not get cold for prolonged periods to avoid getting flu.
7.Name: 健康
7.Title: 健康

8.value:
When you eat raw meat, uncooked eggs or any food that has been allowed to rot, the player may become sick.
 


 Sickness is an unpleasant condition. 
 You health will slowly drop down to a single heart, making you very vulnerable.
 Your activities will be constantly interrupted by vomiting and nausea attacks.
 You will move slowly, be weak and tire easily.
 You will not be able to eat certain food types.
 


 It is best to sleep it off, maybe in the morning your stomach will get better.
8.Name: Sickness
8.Title: Sickness

9.value:
When your body temperature stays low for a long period, the player will start to sneeze.
 This is a warning sign that you may get flu soon.
 Warm yourself up quickly.
 


 Flu is a dangerous condition.
 You health will slowly drop down to two hearts, making you very vulnerable.
 Your activities will be frequently interrupted by coughing attacks.
 You will move slowly, be weak and tire easily.
 You will also become very sleepy.
 


 It is best to get to a warm place and sleep the flu off, maybe in the morning you will get better.
 Flu is very difficult to cure when you are cold.
9.Name: Flu
9.Title: Flu

10.value:
Hunger bar is displayed in the game screen as little fishes.
 If you don't eat for a long time, your character will start complaining about it and eventually losing health, until you die.
 In addition to that, if you're hungry, you don't regenerate health.
 


 To eat, drag an edible item onto the player in the body panel.
 Your character will inform you whether you are still hungry.
 


 Food can be obtained from many sources in the game.
 The obvious one is hunting edible animals, such as cows, birds and wildboars.
 Keep in mind that hunting may be dangerous, and the supply of animals will quickly dry up in nearby areas, forcing you to search further.
 


 An alternative to hunting is farming.
 Farming lets you create a sustainable source of food, although it may require lots of preparatory work and further maintenance.
10.Name: Hunger
10.Title: Hunger

11.value:
Stamina is the measure of tiredness of your character.
 When you perform a tiring action for a long time without break, you will tire and start panting.
 


 When you are panting, your performance is lower than when you are rested.
 You will move and dig slower, and you will not be able to perform jumps.
 


 Some of the actions that are tiring: running, jumping, swimming, digging, fighting.
 


 It is very dangerous to get tired when you are in water, because you can't rest while swimming.
 Watch for early signs of panting, and get out of water immediately.
 When you get exhausted while in water, you will start drowning and die unless the shore is very near.
 


 Be careful, it's easy to get tired when running away from dangerous animals.
11.Name: Estamina
11.Title: Estamina

12.value:
Sleeping will help restore your health and pass the boring night hours when it's too dark or dangerous to work.
 


 Even if you are not hurt, you still need to sleep from time to time.
 If you neglect it, your character will start to have short blackouts.
 If you keep active despite this warning, the blackouts will get longer and eventually you will lose consciousness.
 Losing consciousness is very dangerous, because predatory animals will quickly smell you and feast on your unguarded flesh.
 


 Although you don't need a bed, you can only sleep in a cosy and comfortable place.
 The ground cannot be too hard and the area cannot be too exposed to the elements, or wet.
 


 Your respawn point is set to a place where you last slept.
 To sleep, open the body screen and press a little sleeping button.
 


 Note: Sleeping is disabled in the demo.
12.Name: スリープ
12.Title: スリープ中

13.value:
When you are out in the cold without proper clothes, your body temperature will drop.
 Your view will start to freeze over and you will start losing health.
 Find a warmer place or get near fire to warm and dry yourself.
 If you stay cold for a long time, you will start sneezing and may get flu.
 


 Too much warmth will do you no good either, standing too close to a large fire will cause you to overheat.
 


 Sheltered areas are warmer than outside.
 Plugging all the holes and locking doors will make a shelter warmer still.
 Even a small hole open to the elements will admit draughts and make a large difference to temperature.
 Make a fire inside your shelter to make it homely warm and allow you to take your clothes off.
 


 Being wet dramatically increases your susceptibility to cold, find a warm place to dry yourself faster.
 


 The best way to deal with cold is to wear multiple layers of thick, dry clothes.
 You need to cover your entire body, leaving feet or head unclothed will defeat most of the warming effect other clothes have.
 Clothes made of animal fur are best for keeping cold out.
13.Name: 温度
13.Title: 体温度

14.value:
Your character can get wet by being outside in the rain or by falling into water.
 Wetness heavily reduces insulation provided by clothing, so it's very easy to freeze in colder climates.
 Your character will dry faster in a warm place.
14.Name: 濡れ
14.Title: 濡れ

15.value:
All food items will turn rotten after some time.
 The little white fish icon next to the item will turn brown to indicate that the rot process is starting.
 Some time after that, the food item will rot.
 


 Raw food will rot very quickly.
 Cooking food will prolong its life.
 


 Rotten food is still attractive to animals and can even be eaten by the player, but has a high chance of causing sickness.
 


 When the player is sick, he is very slow, weak and will stop to vomit periodically.
 It's best to go to sleep and wait for the sickness to pass.
 


 Most rotten food will eventually turn into compost, a valuable farming resource which can be used instead of soil to grow plants more quickly.
 This will let you utilize valuable saltpeter for making explosives, instead of sprinkling it as fertilizer.
15.Name: Comercial
15.Title: 食料ロット

16.value:
Your character becomes more experienced over time.
 Experience is represented by little golden orbs that can be picked up.
 They appear when you mine minerals, harvest crops or hunt animals.
 Collect them.
 


 If you gather enough experience, your character will advance to the next level.
 Your strength, speed and resilience will increase and your food needs will decrease.
 You will also be able to use more advanced tools, weapons and armor.
 


 When you die and respawn, your level is reduced to a much lower value.
 It will take a lot of time to regain the lost experience.
16.Name: レベル
16.Title: レベルと経験

17.value:
You can ride certain animals, such as horses or camels.
 Riding an animal lets you move more quickly and view the world from a higher vantage point.
 


 To mount an animal, get close to it and wait for it to stop.
 The sneak button will turn into mount button.
 Press it to mount/dismount.
 You can also mount boats in the same way as animals.
 


 To successfully ride an animal, you need a saddle, which can be crafted from leather and wooden sticks.
 When the animal is saddled, its behavior changes and it becomes more friendly towards you.
 Do not mount an unsaddled animal, it will not like it, and may even hurt you.
 


 A whistle will summon any saddled animals that are nearby.
 It is a good idea to carry a whistle with you when you are riding.
17.Title: 動物に乗る

18.value:
In Survivalcraft, the entire world is destructible.
 


 To dig a block, press it and hold your finger.
 Digging cracks will appear as the block is being dug.
 


 Some blocks take a very long time to dig without proper tools.
 Digging some blocks, such as glass, will destroy them.
18.Title: 掘削ブロック

19.value:
To place a block, select it from your inventory so that you are holding it in your hand.
 Then tap the face of an existing block, where you want the new block to be attached.
 The block will be placed there immediately, unless it is impossible for some reason.
 


 Certain items behave differently if attached to different parts of an existing block.
 For example, trapdoors can be attached to the top or bottom parts with different results.
19.Title: ブロックの配置

20.value:
Certain blocks are interactive.
 Some examples of interactive blocks: doors, chests, workbenches, furnaces, buttons.
 


 To interact with a block, tap on it.
 This will invoke the action associated with the block.
 


 For example, if you tap on a closed door, it will open.
20.Title: ブロックとの相互作用

21.value:
Some blocks are editable.
 Examples of editable blocks: crossbow, memory bank, musket, truth table.
 


 Editable blocks are marked with an edit icon in your inventory.
 


 To edit a block in the inventory, select it so that you hold it in your hand.
 If the selected block is editable, sneak button will turn into edit button.
 


 To edit a block which is placed on the ground, stand close to it and point center of the screen at the block.
 If the block in front of you is editable, it will be highlighted and sneak button will turn into edit button.
 


 Editing lets you change advanced properties of blocks.
 For example editing a crossbow will let you draw it and load a bolt.
 Editing a truth table lets you see and set the binary values.
 To discover which items are editable, watch out for the edit icon on inventory items.
 


 If you both hold an editable item in your hand and look at an editable block in the terrain, the item in the terrain will take precedence.
21.Title: ブロックの編集

22.value:
Certain items can be thrown.
 Some examples of throwable items: stone chunks, spears, bricks, snowballs.
 


 To throw an item, select it in your inventory, so that you are holding it in your hand.
 Press and hold a point on the screen where you want to throw.
 A throwing sights will appear.
 


 You can move the sights to refine your aim.
 Once you release your finger, the item will be thrown.
 To cancel a throw, move the sights to the edge of the screen.
 


 Spears are powerful thrown weapons that do a lot of damage when they hit the target.
22.Title: 投げるアイテム

23.value:
All items and blocks can be dropped if you no longer need them.
 


 To drop an item or block, drag it from the inventory slot and drop it on the screen.
 The item will fly a short distance away and fall to the ground.
 


 Watch out, if you're standing too close you will immediately pick up the dropped item again.
 


 Dropping items into fire or magma will destroy them.
23.Title: アイテムをドロップ

24.value:
Crafting is a process of making more complicated items out of simpler ones, using a workbench or the crafting area.
 


 To craft, you need to place ingredients in the crafting slots in a pattern specific to the item being crafted.
 All crafting recipes are available in RECIPAEDIA.
 


 There are two sizes of crafting areas: 2x2 and 3x3.
 The smaller area is always available and can be accessed by pressing the crafting button.
 To craft more complicated items on a 3x3 area you need to make a workbench, place it somewhere in the world and click on it.
24.Title: クラフト

25.value:
Smelting is thermal processing of substances in a furnace.
 Up to 2 substances can be placed in the furnace at the same time.
 


 Furnace requires fuel to work.
 You can use anything that is combustible as a fuel, but some fuels are better (longer lasting) than others.
 The best fuel is coal.
 


 You can leave the furnace while it is smelting. The smelting process will continue while you are away.
 All smelting recipes are available in RECIPAEDIA.
25.Title: 精錬所

26.value:
Press and hold the slot containing the stack you want to split for about 1 second.
 A red border will appear around it.
 The slot is now in splitting mode.
 Tap any other empty slot, and one item will be taken from the highlighted slot and placed into the tapped slot.
 


 This technique can also be used to quickly spread items from one stack to multiple slots, e.g. in a workbench.
26.Title: アイテムのスタックを分割する

27.value:
You need to mine to obtain resources needed for crafting and smelting.
 Mining can be as simple as digging in a random place with your bare hands.
 You will however soon discover that it is not an effective way to mine, because digging hard rock by hand takes a very long time.
 


 You need to craft mining tools, such as a shovel and pickaxe first.
 You also need to dig in different places to find specific resources.
 Caves are a good starting point, because they show you a large cross section through the terrain for free.
 


 Be aware that some resources, such as diamonds or malachite, require tools made of strong materials to be mined.
27.Title: 採掘中

28.value:
Farming allows you to grow a variety of plants for decorative or consumption purposes.
 To grow a plant, you need to obtain its seeds, usually from a fully grown plant specimen.
 


 Some plants will drop seeds immediately when they are harvested, some will require crafting to extract seeds.
 Some seeds will tolerate poor soil, like dirt or grass.
 Others, like rye or pumpkins, require high quality tilled and moist soil, which has been fertilized with saltpeter.
 To make sure the soil is moist, use bucket to create water channels no further than several blocks away.
 Moist soil is darker than a dry one.
 To further enhance soil quality, sprinkle it with saltpeter (which is a source of nitrogen).
 


 Plants may take a long time to grow, be patient.
28.Title: 農業

29.value:
Buckets are used for transporting fluids, such as water or magma.
 To fill empty bucket with a fluid, you need to find a source block of the fluid.
 For example, all ocean blocks are source blocks of water.
 


 Holding the empty bucket in your hand, tap the source block to collect it.
 The block will be removed from the world and placed inside the bucket.
 


 To pour the fluid from the bucket, tap on terrain.
 A new source block of fluid will be created, and the fluid will spread out from there.
 You can collect the placed source block again with the empty bucket.
29.Title: Bucket

30.value:
Some blocks and items can be dyed or painted.
 


 To paint bricks, stone, wood or other blocks, make a paint bucket of appropriate color and use it on the item you want to paint.
 One bucket is good for painting several dozen blocks, after that it runs out.
 


 Paint stripper can be used to remove paint and restore blocks to their original appearance.
 


 To dye clothing, place a piece of clothing with a bucket full of paint in a furnace.
 You can dye clothing multiple times, combining colors.
 


 You can also dye carpets in a similar way.
 


 Available colors of the paint (palette) can be customized in the world options screen.
 You can also customize the names of colors.
 The total number of colors is always 16, so to make a new color you need to sacrifice one of the existing ones.
 Customized colors also affect clothing, dyeing and furniture.
30.Title: 染料・絵画

31.value:
Electricity is a powerful mechanism in the game that can be used to control behavior of certain blocks.
 For example, a lightbulb will produce light when connected to a source of electricity, doors will open or close if activated with an electric signal, and many more.
 


 If you know a bit about real-world electronics, you should feel at home in Survivalcraft.
 All the familiar devices, such as logic gates (AND, OR, NOT etc.) are present and signals are characterized by voltage.
 You can combine gates to build complex circuits, even computers or video games (check Community Content for some great builds by master electricians).
 There are also sensors, such as pressure plates, photodiodes etc. that produce varying signals depending on conditions around them.
 


 For descriptions of all available electric devices, see RECIPAEDIA.
 


 Electric signals in the game are conducted through copper wires and have a voltage that is between 0V and 1.5V, in increments of 0.1V.
 This gives 16 distinct voltage values.
 


 Digital devices, such as buttons, switches or doors work only with digital signals that have two possible values: low (0, false, off, no) and high (1, true, on, yes).
 In this case, a voltage below 0.8V indicates low, 0.8V and greater indicates high.
 Most commonly digital signals have voltages of 0V for low and 1.5V for high, but any value below 0.8V will be read as low.
 


 Analog devices, such as pressure plates, motion detectors or lightbulbs work with analog signals.
 They will react to each different value of voltage between 0 and 1.5V, not just low and high states.
 For example, sound generator volume and pitch inputs are controlled by analog voltages.
 The volume and pitch will increase smoothly as the voltages get higher.
 


 It's good to remember that both analog and digital signals are really the same thing when conducted through the wire, a voltage between 0V and 1.5V.
 Only the way a device interprets the signals voltage determines whether it is digital or analog.
 


 Voltage can also be interpreted as a number, with 0V representing 0 and 1.5V representing 15.
 Some devices, such as displays, DAC/ADC converters or counters use this interpretation.
 


 All electric devices have connections (inputs and outputs) on their sides, and sometimes also on their back (the face attached to the wall).
 On complex devices these are indicated by blue and red dots on edges of the elements.
 Blue means input, red means output.
 Number of dots means number of the connection.
 Simple devices, such as buttons, switches or lightbulbs do not have these dots, because they use one input/output only and can be connected from any side.
 


 Electric devices can be placed on the walls and on the floor/ceiling (with some exceptions).
 Once placed, they can be rotated so that it's easy to connect appropriate inputs and outputs together with the wires.
 


 There is a special type of block in the game, called wire-though-block, which is a normal block with a hole drilled and an electric wire threaded though it.
 It is very useful when trying to hide wires from sight, run them though the wall, or prevent them from connecting with other wires.
 It is also the only way to access the "back" connection of devices which have it.
 


 Electricity in Survivalcraft is processed at a frequency of 100Hz.
 It means that voltage goes from one element to another in 0.01s, irrespective of the length of the wire.
 If you need to delay the signal, you may place some neutral elements in its path (for example an OR gate or a pair of NOT gates).
 Each additional element in the way of the signal will delay it by 0.01s.
 For longer delays use Adjustable Delay Gate, which can produce delays of up to 2.55s.
 


 Electricity is a complex topic and making large working machines requires some learning, but is very satisfying.
 Simple devices however can be constructed by anyone.
 For example, try placing a button next to the hinges of a door.
 You will notice a wire appeared, clicking the button will now operate the door!
 All this scary talk about voltages, signals, digital and analog, but in reality it's that simple.
 


 Another easy thing to build might be a light switch.
 Place a lightbulb on the ceiling and a switch (not button) on the wall.
 Then connect them with a wire.
 The switch now operates the light, like in a real room!
 


 Survivalcraft is more about realism than other games of this type, and electricity is no exception.
 What you learn here, might become invaluable when you get to learn this at school, or have to use it for living.
 


 Explore and have fun!
31.Title: 電気

32.value:
In Survivalcraft you can design and use custom furniture blocks.
 


 Furniture blocks are sculpted from up to 16x16x16 smaller sub-blocks.
 Sub-blocks within a single furniture block can be made of different materials and painted to different colors.
 Through crafting, furniture blocks can be combined, made interactive or made to work with electricity.
 Furniture designs can be combined into packs, shared between worlds and uploaded to Community Content.
 


 To design and create furniture use the hammer tool.
 You need to build the design from regular blocks in the world and then use the hammer on the resulting structure to shrink it down.
 Furniture design window will appear where you can tweak the design by moving it, rotating it and changing resolution (between 2x2x2 and 16x16x16).
 Once you are happy with the design, you can commit it.
 


 In creative mode, the design will be turned into a single furniture block, and the source blocks from which the design was created will remain in the world.
 In survival modes, the design will be turned into several copies of the furniture block (with number depending on resolution) and the source blocks will be consumed.
 Furniture blocks can be placed in the world in 4 orientations, similar to e.g. a furnace or a ladder.
 


 Not every block can be used for furniture design.
 For example, dirt or grass are ignored by the hammer tool.
 Allowed materials include: planks, granite, basalt, sand, clay, cobblestone, stone bricks, sandstone, marble, bricks, coal, malachite, diamond, copper, iron, glass, carpet, torches, wicker lamps, water, magma.
 Source blocks of the design can be painted or dyed to make the design more colorful.
 Finished furniture can also be painted, this will cover any colors of the source blocks.
 


 Certain source blocks have special functions.
 For example a torch/lamp block will cause the furniture to have a fire attached to it in the place of the block.
 The design will also emit light.
 


 You have to be careful when constructing furniture to avoid placing the design on a base made of allowed material (for example sand).
 The hammer tool will not be able to distinguish between the blocks you placed and the base, and will try to incorporate base into the design.
 This will likely cause "design too large" error, because the maximum size of a design in any dimension is 16 blocks.
 


 Once the furniture block has been created, you can use workbench to combine it with other blocks in various ways.
 The following combinations are possible:
 


 * Combine multiple pieces of furniture into one to create interactive furniture.
 Interactive furniture toggles between its constituing designs on click or on electric signal.
 You can create opening doors, switchable lights, raising platforms etc.
 Up to 9 pieces of furniture can be combined (max capacity of workbench).
 


 * Combine exactly one furniture design with an electric button to create a custom button.
 The resulting furniture will act as a button.
 


 * Combine exactly two furniture designs with an electric switch to create a custom switch.
 The resulting furniture will act as a switch, with two furniture pieces acting as two states of the switch.
 


 * Combine multiple pieces of furniture with an electric wire to create connected interactive furniture.
 The furniture will work in the same way as interactive furniture, but toggling it will also toggle all neighboring pieces of connected interactive furniture.
 You can create multi-block toggleable structures, for example large doors.
 


 In the furniture panel in creative inventory you can view and organize your furniture designs.
 Create named furniture sets to categorize the designs.
 The newly created designs are placed in "Uncategorized" set, which has a special function.
 Any design in this set, which does not otherwise exist in the world (no items or placed blocks) will be automatically recycled.
 Be careful and move valuable designs to named furniture sets to avoid accidentally losing them.
 Each named furniture set can be turned into a furniture pack and exported from the world.
 You can later import it to other worlds or upload to Dropbox/SDcard and share with others.
 Furniture packs can also be uploaded to community content.
 


 There are certain limitations to the furniture system:
 


 * A furniture block can be made from up to 16x16x16 sub-blocks.
 


 * The amount of geometry used for a single design cannot exceed 300 triangles (too complex designs with many nooks and crannies are not possible).
 The game uses an intelligent tesselation algorithm that optimizes the geometry, so 300 triangles is plenty for even fairly complex designs, unless you really go over the top.
 To reduce number of triangles, you can reduce number of small details, reduce number of painted blocks and fill up any internal holes in the design.
 


 * You can only have up to 1024 unique furniture designs in the world.
 Every time you paint a furniture or combine it with others, you are creating new designs that count towards the total.
 Unused designs (that do not exist in the world either as items or blocks, and are in the "uncategorized" set in the inventory) are automatically recycled.
32.Title: 家具

33.value:
Pistons are blocks that can push or pull other blocks. 
 Pistons will extend or retract based on voltage applied to them.
 They can be placed facing any direction.
 


 There are 3 types of pistons: push pistons, pull pistons and strict pull pistons.
 


 Push pistons push up to 8 blocks as they extend, but they do not pull them back when they retract.
 


 Pull pistons act like push pistons, but additionally they pull up to 8 blocks back when they retract.
 


 Strict pull pistons act like normal pull pistons, but they will not retract unless they are pulling enough blocks.
 


 Voltage applied to a piston determines the extension length.
 Anthing below 0.8V is retracted. 0.8V is one block extension, 0.9V is two blocks etc.
 1.5V equals full extension of 8 blocks.
 The piston will only react to voltage changes when it is not moving.
 It will always complete the current move before starting another.
 


 Pistons can be edited.
 You can adjust maximum extension, number of pulled blocks and speed of movement.
 


 Certain blocks cannot be moved by pistons.
 These include any block that contains inventory (chest, workbench, furnace etc.), diamond block and any piston that is extended.
 Retracted pistons however can be moved by other pistons.
 This opens up a world of possibilities, with complicated machines where pistons move other pistons.
 Pistons will also block fluids when extended, so you can use them to make valves.
 


 Use pistons to make doors, elevators, moving platforms and all kinds of ingenious machines.
33.Title: Pistons

34.value:
Wearing clothing provides warmth in cold climates and armor protection during combat.
 You need heavy clothing to survive for any period of time in cold areas of the world.
 


 The level of thermal insulation is measured in CLO units.
 For example, 0.0 CLO is naked body, 1.0 CLO is light clothing, 3.0 CLO is heavy insulation.
 


 You can access clothing interface by using Clothing button.
 There are 4 clothing slots on your body: head, torso, legs and feet.
 Each slot can have multiple clothing items, layered one on top of each other.
 The effects of multiple clothing items combine.
 


 Not everything can be worn on top of everything else, for example you will not be able to wear a t-shirt on top of a fur coat.
 


 Wearing thick clothes in one slot while leaving another slot bare will not afford much insulation.
 It's imperative to cover entire body to really benefit from clothing.
 It's the thinnest piece of clothing that determines how much heat the character loses.
34.Name: 服
34.Title: 服

35.value:
Wearing armor or thick clothing will reduce or even completely eliminate combat injuries.
 Armor will absorb the hits, so that they damage the armor instead of injuring your body.
 Once the armor receives too much damage, it will disintegrate.
 


 When you are attacked, you will randomly receive a hit into one of your four body areas: head, torso, legs or feet.
 If there is no armor at that slot, you will receive full injury, irrespective of how much armor you wear elsewhere.
 By far the most likely area to receive hits is torso, followed by legs.
 Wearing torso armor will stop 60% of hits, torso and legs together will stop 80%.
 Head and feet armor is of less importance, unless you want to be 100% safe.
 


 The strongest armor is made of iron and diamonds.
 Early in the game, you can craft wooden torso armor from sticks and strings.
 It's not very sturdy but will stand a few hits potentially saving your life.
 


 Even plain clothes have certain armor value, especially thick ones made of leather and fur.
 They do not stop all hits, but only partially reduce the damage.
 


 Wearing heavy armor will make you sink to the bottom and drown, be careful around deep water.
35.Title: よろいかぶと

36.value:
Boats can be used to travel across large bodies of water quickly and safely.
 


 If you don't have a boat, you risk drowning from exhaustion or being attacked by dangerous underwater creatures.
 


 To craft a boat you need lots of wood and a workbench.
 Once the boat is crafted, it can be placed on the shore and mounted in the same way as a horse.
 


 You can only control a boat when it is in the water.
36.Title: ボート

37.value:
Sometimes when you are stuck, the only option is to force respawn at your last sleeping place.
 


 To do it, open the body screen and repeatedly press the skull and bones button.
37.Title: リスポーンを強制する

38.value:
The game uses Dropbox service to allow you to download and upload data to/from your device.
 


 Through Dropbox, you can backup and share worlds with others.
 You can also download custom blocks textures, character skins, furniture packs.
 


 To login to Dropbox, click on the login button on the Dropbox screen.
 If you don't have a Dropbox account yet, you will be able to setup one during the login.
 


 Once you log in, the Dropbox screen will show you the contents of your online Dropbox folders, and allow you to download the files to your device.
 


 See https://dropbox.com website for more details.
38.Title: Dropbox

39.value:
The game uses transfer.sh anonymous file service to allow you to upload data from your device without the hassle of creating any user accounts.
 


 Use the link provided after the upload to access the data.
 Transfer.sh keeps the data for a few days only, after that it is automatically deleted.
 


 Do not use these temporary links for Community Content uploads, your item will be deleted by the service and Community Content item will stop working.
 


 It is not possible to see the list of items you uploaded, the sole access to data is through the links.
 


 See https://transfer.sh website for more details.
 


 Big Kudos to The Dutch Coders!
39.Title: Transfer.sh

40.value:
The game allows you to import and export worlds to SD Card for sharing and backup purposes.
 


 If your device does not have an SD Card, generic external storage folder will be used.
 The location of the data on the device is normally /sdcard/Survivalcraft
40.Title: SD カード

41.value:
The game allows you to browse a list of worlds, block textures and other items published by other Survivalcraft community members, and download them.
 


 Once you make something interesting, you can also publish it through this service, and it will appear on the list for anyone to download.
 


 To publish something, first you need to put it on the internet, so that it's accessible to everyone.
 The easiest place to use is Dropbox, although you can use any other provider.
 Once it's done, you can publish the link to it through "Publish Link" dialog in the game.
 


 Note: the server does not store your content, only the link.
 Just like a search engine.
 You are responsible for keeping the data accessible through the published link.
 Watch out for exceeding bandwidth limits of your provider, having a link near top of the list will make your content receive many thousands of downloads in a short time.
41.Title: コミュニティコンテンツ

Strings.GameMode.Creative.Description: クリエイティブモードでは、すべてのアイテムが無限に供給され、すべてのブロックは無限に高速で回収できます。 あなたはまた不滅であり、飛ぶことができます。
Strings.GameMode.Harmless.Description: ハームレスモードでは、ほとんどすべての危険がゲームから削除されます。動物はunprovekedを攻撃しません。 滝は致命的ではありません。体力の回復は非常に速いです。掘るのが25%速いです。空中にいる間はブロックを置くことができます。
Strings.GameMode.Survival.Description: サバイバルはデフォルトのモードです。危険がありますが、死ぬと最後の睡眠時にリスポーンできます。 簡単すぎると思ったら、チャレンジモードをお試しください。
Strings.GameMode.Challenging.Description: チャレンジは高度なサバイバルモードです。危険はありますが、死ぬと最後の睡眠時にリスポーンできます。
Strings.GameMode.Cruel.Description: 残酷モードはチャレンジモードと同じですが、リスポーンすることはできません。 一度死んでしまったら、あなたの世界は失われます！
Strings.GameMode.Adventure.Description: アドベンチャーモードはクエストマップを作成し、他のプレイヤーと共有したいプレイヤーのためのモードです。 このモードでは、ツールなしで掘ることはできませんし、健康は再生しません。 クリエイティブモードでクエストマップを作成し、アップロードする前にアドベンチャーに変更してください。
Strings.SeaLevelOffset.Description: 世界の海面を調整します。
Strings.TemperatureOffset.Description: 世界の気温を調整します。
Strings.HumidityOffset.Description: 世界の全球湿度を調整します。
Strings.EnvironmentBehaviorMode.Living.Descripti: リビングモードでは、世界は動物に満ちた生活の場所です。 植物や草は、緩やかな材料の崩壊と切り離された葉で作られたブロックが成長します。
Strings.EnvironmentBehaviorMode.Static.Descripti: 静的モードでは、世界に動物はいません。植物の成長、ブロックの崩壊、植物の崩壊は無効になります。 自分の世界を自分の好きなように形作る場合に便利です。
Strings.TimeOfDayMode.Changing.Description: 太陽、月、星は空を横切って移動します。
Strings.TimeOfDayMode.Day.Description: 時刻は常に正午になります。 太陽、月、星は空に静止します。
Strings.TimeOfDayMode.Night.Description: 昼の時間は常に真夜中になります, 太陽, 月や星は空に静止滞在します.
Strings.TimeOfDayMode.Sunrise.Description: 太陽、月、星は、空に静止状態にとどまります。
Strings.TimeOfDayMode.Sunset.Description: 太陽、月、星は空に静止します。
Strings.AreSeasonsChanging.False: ワールドは選択されたシーズンにロックされます。気象条件は変更されません。
Strings.AreSeasonsChanging.True: 季節は定期的に変化します。季節が進むにつれ、天候はゆっくりと変化します。
Strings.YearDays.Description: すべての季節のサイクルにかかる日数を調整します。各シーズンはこの値の4分の1を持続します。
Strings.TimeOfYear.Description: 世界の季節を調整します。
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Continent.Name: コンチネンタル
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Continent.Descript: コンチネンタルモードでは、環境は森林に満ちた現実世界のように見えるように作られています。 平野部、砂漠、氷河、湖、山、丘、洞窟などです
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Island.Name: 島
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Island.Description: Islandモードでは、地形は小さな島に制限されていますが、それ以外の場合は大陸モードのように見えます。島のサイズを調整することもできます。
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.FlatContinent.Name: 平坦なコンチネンタル
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.FlatContinent.Desc: フラットモードでは、地形は完全に平坦であり、創造の完全な自由を与えます。 また、地形レベルやブロックの種類を変更して埋めることもできます。
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.FlatIsland.Name: 平らな島
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.FlatIsland.Descrip: フラットモードでは、地形は完全に平坦であり、創造の完全な自由を与えます。 また、地形レベルやブロックの種類を変更して埋めることもできます。
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Nihility.Name: Nihility
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Nihility.Descripti: ブロックがありません。
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Better.Name: 地形の最適化
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Better.Description: 真の新しい盆地地形、より多くの自然の洞窟や川、全体的な地形に近い現実世界に。
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Huge.Name: 巨大な
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Huge.Description: 警告:デバイスの性能要件が非常に高くなります!世界中の高さの制限は512に増加し、より壮大な山々とバイオームがあります。
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Cave.Name: Frozen Plateau 256
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Cave.Description: 全世界は凍結した台地で覆われており、クモの巣のようなカルスト洞窟が交差しています。 土と植生は非常に不足しており、あなたの生存限界に挑戦します。
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.SkyIsland.Name: スカイアイランドサバイバルformat@@0
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.SkyIsland.Descript: 島の世界は、空に浮かんで、すべてのステップは、高高度絶望的な状況で生き残るために苦労して注意が必要です。
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Bedrock.Name: 岩盤大陸（岩盤）
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Bedrock.Descriptio: 神々の戦争の後に粉砕大陸、岩盤で覆われた表面、環境極端で危険。
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Stone.Name: 石のコンチネンタル
Strings.TerrainGenerationMode.Stone.Description: 世界全体が様々な岩に完全に包まれています。
Strings.IslandSize.Description: 島のサイズを調整します。
Strings.BiomeSize.Description: バイオームの大きさを調整します。
Strings.FlatTerrainLevel.Description: 平坦な地形モードでのみ適用可能な岩盤上の地形のレベルを調整します。 下に置くと、より多くのスペースが地上で利用可能になります。それを置くほど、より多くのスペースが地下で利用可能になります。
Strings.FlatTerrainShoreRoughness.Description: 平坦な地形の海岸の荒さを調整し、平坦地形モードでのみ適用できます。 ゼロは完全に真っ直ぐな海岸です。値が高いほど、海岸線は荒くなります。
Strings.FlatTerrainMagmaOcean.Description: 海をマグマの海に変えます。
Strings.WeatherMode.True: 世界は、地面に蓄積する雨や雪の定期的な発生を体験します。 稲妻は、背の高い物体を襲い、火をつけます。
Strings.WeatherMode.False: 世界では降水量や雷はありません。
Strings.AdventureRespawnMode.True: プレイヤーは最後のスポーン地点または寝場所でリスポーンすることができます(チェックポイント付きの長い冒険に最適です)。
Strings.AdventureRespawnMode.False: プレイヤーはリスポーンできません しかし、彼が死ぬとき、冒険を再開し、ゼロから始める必要があります(チェックポイントのない小さな冒険に適しています)。
Strings.AdventureSurvivalMechanics.True: 飢餓、スタミナ、体温、睡眠遮断は冒険モードでシミュレートされます。
Strings.AdventureSurvivalMechanics.False: 飢餓、スタミナ、体温、睡眠遮断は冒険モードで無効になります。健康と怪我はまだシミュレートされます。
Strings.SupernaturalCreatures.True: 超自然的な生き物は世界に出現します。
Strings.SupernaturalCreatures.False: 超自然的な生き物は世界に出現しません。既存の生き物はすべて放棄されたまま残ります。
Strings.FriendlyFire.True: プレイヤーは他のプレイヤーを攻撃し、怪我させることができます。
Strings.FriendlyFire.False: 他のプレイヤーを攻撃したり傷つけたりすることはできません。
Strings.CharacterSkin.Description: ゲーム中に使用するキャラクタースキンを選択します。
Strings.BlocksTexture.Description: 世界を描くときに使うブロックのテクスチャを選択します。異なるブロックテクスチャを使うことで、世界の外観を完全に変えることができます。
Strings.Settings.Compatibility.SinglethreadedTer: これを有効にすると、地形を更新したときにのみ単一スレッドを使用するようにゲームを強制します。 マルチコア・デバイスではゲームの動作が遅くなりますが、単一コアデバイスではゲームの動作が若干遅くなる可能性があります。
Strings.Settings.Compatibility.UseAudioTrackCach: オーディオに問題が発生し、Android 4.3 以前の場合は、この設定を有効にすると、作成された AudioTrack オブジェクトの数が減ります。
プレイヤーの近くの見えない部分や文字化けしたテクスチャなどのグラフィックで問題が発生した場合は、これを有効にしてください。 一部のAndroidデバイスでは、全範囲でデプスバッファリングやクリッピングの問題が発生するため、クリップスペースのZ範囲が-1/1から0/1に低減されます。
Strings.Settings.Compatibility.AllowLanConnectio: これを有効にすると、他のデバイスがLAN経由でゲームサーバーに接続できるようになります。無効にすると、すべてのネットワーク接続機能が無効になります。
Strings.ServerConfig.Password.Description: ルームパスワードを設定します。誰でも参加できます。 パスワードを設定すると、正しいパスワードを入力したプレイヤーのみがサーバーに参加できます。
Strings.ServerConfig.MaxPlayers.Description: 同時にオンラインにできるプレイヤーの最大数を設定します。範囲：1-20。値が高いほど、サーバーのパフォーマンスが向上します。
Strings.ServerConfig.NeedLogin.Description: プレイヤーがサーバーに参加する前にコミュニティアカウントにログインする必要があるかどうかを設定します。 有効にすると、プレイヤーの身元を管理し、悪意のある行動を防ぐことができます。
Strings.ServerConfig.RandomSpawn.Description: ランダムスポーン地点を有効にするかどうか。有効にすると、新たに参加したプレイヤーは固定のスポーン地点ではなくランダムな位置にスポーンします。
Strings.ServerConfig.DaySpeed.Description: 昼/夜のサイクル速度を設定します。1.0は通常の速度、2.0は半速、10です。 はスピードが最も遅いです。昼/夜のサイクルを遅くすると、プレイヤーは建築や探索に時間がかかります。
Strings.ServerConfig.RecoverySpeed.Description: 睡眠時の回復速度係数を設定します。睡眠中の回復速度が速くなります。最小値は0.1です。
Strings.ServerConfig.DisableBlocks.Description: 無効にするブロックのリストを設定します。セミコロン(;)で複数のブロックIDを分離します。無効にしたブロックは配置または使用できません。例: 1;2;3
Strings.ServerConfig.KeywordBlocking.Description: ブロックするキーワードのリストを設定します。複数のキーワードをセミコロン（;）で区切ってください。これらのキーワードを含むチャットメッセージは自動的にフィルタリングされます。
Strings.ServerConfig.Reset.Description: すべてのサーバー構成がデフォルト値にリセットされました。

NetMessageWidget.ChatHelpContent:
Interface Interaction
The Message, Party, and Activity panels, as well as top-right controls, now support "Auto-bring to front on click/activation" to resolve overlapping issues.
Messaging System
Sends global messages by default.
Initial red text is hidden by default; the system automatically blocks prohibited text.
Clicking a selected message again brings up copy/action options.
The input text box supports horizontal dragging; on PC, it additionally supports instant cursor positioning upon clicking.
Notification sounds and bottom message overlays can be adjusted in Settings.
Ctrl+Backspace to delete content to the left of the cursor.
Commands: Type / at the start of a line to send supported instructions (varies by server).
Simple Rich Text Syntax
Note: Due to legacy reasons, nesting is not supported. To edit non-text elements, use backspace on their right side.
Space: \
,
Color: \Green Text\,
Block: \8\,
Emoji: \smile\
Use \\ as an escape character for special symbols: \<, \>, \\.
Party System
Supports broadcasting party events to member message queues, friendly fire toggles, member invitation permissions, and member kick permissions.
Leaders can transfer leadership to a member by selecting them in the party list first.

Description.1: 草を食べる大きな哺乳類。通常は攻撃的ではないが、近づきすぎる人は誰も気に入らない。 牛に向かって非常に保護. 激怒したときに危険. 食べておいしい.
Description.2: 鞍が装備されている場合、騎乗することができる従順な動物。一度悲しむと、笛に反応します。非常に速く動き、攻撃的ではありません。
Description.3: 大きく危険な哺乳類の雑食動物。近づくものは何でも追いかける。かなりゆっくりだが強力だ。 黒いクマとは異なり、暖かいエリアが好きです。食べられませんが、狩られると毛皮を提供します。
Description.4: 中規模の淡水魚、積極的ではありません食べておいしい。
Description.5: 草を食べる哺乳類。バケツで搾乳できます。牛乳はしばらくすると再生されます。茶色の牛よりも早く生成されます。 攻撃されたとき、時にはその地面に立つことに決めます. しばしば近くの牛に保護されます. 食べておいしい.
Description.6: 深海に生息する非常に大きく攻撃的でない哺乳類。狩りをするとたくさんの食料が手に入ります。
Description.7: 飛ぶことのない大きな鳥。攻撃的ではないが、非常に速く走る。時には大きな食べられる卵を産む。
Description.8: 中型の猫。パワフルで速く、非常に高いジャンプが可能。 追跡を開始すると、それから逃げることはほとんど不可能です. ジャガーやタイガーよりも小さいです.
Description.9: 大規模なカモシカ. 通常攻撃的ではないが、攻撃されれば自分自身を守ることができます.
Description.10: 寒くて温帯に生息する中型の捕食性哺乳類。 夜には、パックで速く移動し、狩りをします。時々停止し、月に頭を高く上げて吠えます。 日中は恥ずかしがり屋であり、トラブルから離れて移動します. 夜は非常に危険です, 大量に遭遇したとき. 大きい距離から獲物のにおいを嗅ぎ、それを狩るために集まるであろう。 満月でWerewolfに変わる。
Description.11: 巨大な植物は哺乳類を食べます。孤独、温帯性、強力です。装甲肌を持っています。激怒すると非常に簡単にイライラし、非常に危険です。 理由もなく、追いかけて、近づくものは何でも踏みつぶすであろうし、暖かいエリアに住む。
Description.12: 中規模の植物は哺乳類を食べます. 恐ろしい蟻のペアにもかかわらず、Docile. 寒い地域で群れに住んでいます. 食べておいしい.
Description.13: 中型の捕食塩水魚。痛みを伴う噛み傷がある。食べるのがおいしい。暖かい地域でのみ見つけられる。
Description.14: 寒い場所に住んでいる小さな鳥。時々食べられる卵を産む。狩られたときに食べるのがおいしい。
Description.15: サドルを装備した場合、騎乗することができる大きな動物。一度サドルを装備すると、ホイッスルに反応します。 速く動き、気質が高く、穏やかに攻撃的。砂漠に住んでいる。
Description.16: 満月の夜、オオカミは人狼に変わります。その赤い光沢のある目で夜に簡単に見ることができます。 非常に危険な、パックで狩り、通常のオオカミよりもはるかに強いです。高いジャンプすることができます。夜明けにオオカミに戻ります。
Description.17: 深海に生息する非常に大きな捕食性の哺乳類。非常に強く危険な状態で動くものは何でも追いかけます。狩りをすると大量の食料が手に入ります。
Description.18: 湿気の多い場所に住む小さな鳥。時々食べられる卵を産む。狩られたときに食べるのがおいしい。
Description.19: 非常に大きく背の高い植物は哺乳類を食べます。非常に強いが従順です。危険なときに逃げる代わりに自分自身を守ることにしました。 暖かい地域に住んでいます. おいしい食べて肉をたくさん提供します.
Description.20: 小さくて攻撃的な海水サメ。近くに来るものは何でも追いかける。非常に強くないが、小さい動物を簡単に殺す。
Description.21: 大規模で攻撃的な海水サメ。近くに来るものは何でも追いかけるぞ。 非常に強く、簡単にほとんどすべての陸上動物を殺すことができます。クジラだけがその猛烈な攻撃に耐えることができます。
Description.22: 大きく危険な哺乳類の雑食動物。近づくものは何でも追いかける。かなりゆっくりだが強力だ。 褐色のクマとは異なり、寒い地域が好きです。食べられませんが、狩られると毛皮を提供します。
Description.23: 大きな猫。強力で速く、夜に狩りをするのが好き。ヒョウやジャガーよりも大きいが、タイガーと同じくらい危険ではない。
Description.24: 非常に大きな猫。非常に強力で、高速で、非常に高いジャンプが可能です。 追跡が始まったら逃げることはほとんど不可能である。 他のどの猫よりも大きくて強い。Rhinocerosのような非常に大きく装甲した動物だけがその攻撃を生き残ることができます。
Description.25: サドルを装備した場合、騎乗することができる頑固な動物。一度サドルを装備すると、ホイッスルに反応します。 急速に動き、攻撃的ではなく、転倒に対して非常に強靱です。 時々頑固さのフィットを持っていて、移動を拒否します。それを供給すると、しばしば問題が解決します。
Description.26: 大きな草を食べる哺乳類。バケツで搾乳できます。牛乳はしばらくすると再生され、黒い牛より遅くなります。 攻撃されたとき、時にはその地面に立つことに決めます. しばしば近くの牛に保護されます. 食べておいしい.
Description.27: 非常に攻撃的で、パックで狩猟される小さな捕食魚。 空腹のピラニアのパックは、水の中に歩くほとんどすべての生き物の短い仕事をするでしょう。 食べられません。暖かい場所だけで生活できます。
Description.28: 中規模の植物を食べる哺乳類。そのサイズはかなり攻撃的だが、他の動物ほど速く動かない。ソリティア。食べるのがおいしい。
Description.29: 大きくて危険な哺乳類の雑食動物。近づくものは何でも追いかける。より速く、より大きく、より強力なクマ。非常に寒い場所にのみ生息する。 食べられません, しかし、狩猟時に毛皮を提供します. 特別な足のパディングは、それが他の生き物よりもはるかに少ない滑らせるため、氷の上で非常に危険.
Description.30: 底に生息する魚, 塩と淡水の両方に住んでいます. しばしば頭だけ突き出して底の泥の中に自分自身を埋葬します. 攻撃的ではありません, しかし、深刻にそれを踏む誰にでも負傷します毒刺しを持っています 食べることがおいしい.
Description.31: 恐ろしい角を持つ大きな草を食べる哺乳類。通常は攻撃的ではないが、攻撃されるとしばしば自身を守ります。 強力で回復力があり小さな捕食者の群れを怖がらせることがよくあります 寒い場所に住んでいます. おいしい食べると狩り時に貴重な毛皮を提供します.
Description.32: 底に生息する魚, 塩と淡水の両方に住んでいます. しばしば頭だけ突き出して底の泥の中に自分自身を埋葬します. ではない積極的な、有毒な刺されていない、黄色のスティングとは異なり、食べておいしい。
Description.33: 砂漠に生息する中規模の捕食動物。夜にはパックで狩りをする。 時々停止し、月に高くその頭を上げて吠えるでしょう。 日中は恥ずかしがり屋であり、トラブルから離れて移動します. 夜は非常に危険です, 大量に遭遇したとき. 大きな距離から獲物の匂いを嗅ぎ、それを狩るために集まります。
Description.34: 中規模の海水魚、積極的ではありません食べておいしい。
Description.35: 小さくて攻撃的で不快な捕食性の哺乳類。夜はパックで狩りをする。 日中は恥ずかしがり屋で、トラブルから離れて滞在することを好みます.
Description.36: 海辺に住んでいる小さな鳥。時々食べられる卵が産まれる。狩られたときに食べるのがおいしい。
Description.37: 草を食べる哺乳類の非常にまれなアルビノ突然変異。通常は攻撃的ではないが、近くに近づく人は好きではない。 激怒したときに危険にさらされます。食べるのがおいしいです。通常の牛よりも多くの肉を提供します。
Description.38: 巨大で攻撃的な海のサメ。近くに来るものは何でも追いかけるだろう。 非常に良い匂いを持っており、遠くからその獲物に家に帰る。 非常に強く、すべての陸上動物を簡単に殺すことができます。クジラだけがその猛烈な攻撃に耐えることができます。
Description.39: 草を食べる哺乳類。保護のために群れの中に住んでいます。攻撃的ではありませんが、近づきすぎる人は好きではありません。 強力で危険なとき. おいしい食べると狩り時に貴重な毛皮を提供します.
Description.40: 中型の猫。パワフルで速く、非常に高いジャンプが可能。 追跡を開始すると、それから逃げることはほとんど不可能です. ヒョウより大きく、強く, しかし、タイガーほど大きくはありません.
Description.41: 中規模の植物を食べる哺乳類。とても恥ずかしがり屋ではない。食べるのがおいしい。
Description.42: 大きなフライトのない鳥。攻撃的で鋭い爪があり、警戒のない攻撃者に壊滅的な傷害を与える可能性があります。時には大きな食べられる卵を産みます。
Description.43: 女性は速く動き、多くの食べ物を必要としないが、男性よりも弱く、多くの怪我をして生き残ることはできない。
Description.44: 男は強いです, ハードヒットし、多くの負傷に耐えることができます, しかし、彼は女性よりも遅い移動であり、生き残るために、より多くの食べ物を必要とします.
Description.46: 温帯に住む小さな鳥。時々小さくて食べられる卵を産む。食べられない。
Description.47: 温帯に生息する小さな鳥。とても小さいが食用の卵を産むこともある。食べられないこともある。
